sources.translation:
Copy. To Boukolos, ex-agoranomus, ex-priest of Alexander
                         founder of the city and of the classes, priest, chief-judge, and curator of the chrematistae
                         and the other courts, through his son, Sarapion, also ex-priest of Alexander founder of
                         the city and of the classes, temporarily performing the duties of chief-judge, from Herakleia
                         also called Isidora, daughter of Mysthes, who is absent and on whose behalf her brother,
                         Ptolemaios, son of Mysthes, is acting by virtue of an agreement done through the registrar’s
                         office at Ptolemais Euergetis in the Arsinoite nome, in the month of Epeiph of the last
                         year, and from Ptolemaios himself and Lysimachos, son of Didymos, son of Lysimachos,
                         whose mother is Apia, and from Herakleides, son of Sarapion, son of Herakleides, all of
                         them from the metropolis of the Arsinoite nome.
                 Whereas Herakleides has been married without written contract with Apia also called
                         Herakleia, daughter of Herakleia also called Isidora, and the latter’s deceased husband,
                         Kronion, brother of Lysimachos, and as during the marriage she became pregnant through
                         him and was delivered, the child - - -; (whereas), moreover, Apia also called Herakleia
                         having died, Herakleia also called Isidora and her said brother, Ptolemaios, and Lysimachos
                         and (on the other side) Herakleides have attended before Eudemos, strategos of the Herakleides
                         district in the Arsinoite nome, the party of Herakleia also called Isidora argued that
                         the child borne by Apia also called Herakleia had died and that Herakleides had adopted
                         another one;  Herakleides himself, however, argued that the child which he had taken
                         with him was really the child born by Apia also called Herakleia, as appears according
                         to their own information from the record drawn up in the presence of the strategos in
                         the month of Epeiph of the 4th year of Hadrian the lord, which proves also that the case
                         has been left to the higher court.  Since it has happened, as both parties concede, that the
                         infant itself, either of Apia also called Herakleia or a strange one, has died, they have now
                         settled the whole controversy and agree with each other, viz. Herakleia also called Isidora
                         and Ptolemaios and Lysimachos, that they will not proceed against Herakleides neither
                         on account of the charge taken down in the minutes of the strategos nor of any other
                         matter with or without written
               
            
            
               
                   document from past times until today; and the other
                           party, Herakleides, that he will proceed neither against Herakleia also called Isidora and
                           Ptolemaios and Lysimachos nor against any of the possessions left by Apia also called
                           Herakleia, neither on the strength of the minutes nor of any other - - -;  Herakleia also
                           called Isidora and Ptolemaios and Lysimachos suffering no loss in any other debt contracted
                           with or without a written document. We request.  (Date).
                           Translation: B.A. van Groningen, P.Fam. Tebt. 20

sources.description: Gift | Didymarion receives 600 dr. and gifts from mother

sources.translation:
The 6th year of Imperator Caesar Trajanus Hadrianus Augustus,
                      the month of Kaisareios 1st, at Tebtunis in the Polemon district of the Arsinoite nome.
                Didymarion, daughter of Herakleides, aged about 48 years, having no distinguishing
                      marks, with the assistance of Kronion, son of Lysanias, her nephew on mother’s side, aged
                      about 52 years, having a scar on the left side of the neck, acknowledges to her brothers
                      from the same father and the same mother, Herakleides also called Valerius, aged about
                      44 years, having a scar on the right knee, and Lysimachos, aged about 38 years, having
                      a scar on the left knee, in the presence of our common mother, Didyme, daughter of Lysimachos,
                      aged about 75 years, having a scar on the left brow, with the assistance of one
                      of her sons, Lysimachos,  that Didymarion has received from her two brothers from hand
                      to hand the 600 silver drachmae promised by our common father, Herakleides, son of Maron,
                      who is dead, beside those (valuables) which he (gave) her through the marriage-contract
                      drawn up by the same registrar’s office in the 9th year of the deified Trajan, above the
                      dowry of 500 silver drachmae which he granted her through the same marriage-contract,
                      the full total being 1100 drachmae, and through an agreement of the father in the 10th
                      year of the deified Trajan, 6th supplementary day through the same office;  and neither
                      Didymarion nor her representatives shall proceed against the two brothers nor against
                      one of them nor against their representatives, neither on account of these (payments),
                      nor on account of any other written or unwritten (obligation) or debt or any agreement
                      whatsoever - - - from the past times until the present day, without any excuse.  And
                      moreover, Didyme, their mother, agrees that she has forthwith endowed her daughter,
                      Didymarion, with a pair of golden earrings with genuine pearls of four quarters (in weight)
                      and a purple garment to be worn with (?) one tunic, all which gifts Didymarion has received;
              and on compulsion Didyme herself shall guarantee immediately for her daughter
                      Didymarion as neither alienated nor encumbered in any way the half of the house and
                      courtyard in Tebtunis which she already gave her through the said contract dated in the
                      10th year of Trajan, 6th supplementary day.  (Subscriptions of i. Didymarion, written by
                      Kronion,  ii. of Lysimachos and Didyme, written by Lysimachos,  iii. of Herakleides-Valerius.
              Docket of registrar).
                      Translation: B.A. van Groningen, P.Fam. Tebt. 21

sources.greek: ἔτους ἑβδόμου Αὐτοκράτορος Καίσαρος Τραιανοῦ Ἁδριανοῦ Σεβαστοῦ μηνὸς Σεβαστοῦ εἰκάδι ἐν ΤεβτύνειΤεβτύνι τῆς Πολέμωνος μερίδος τοῦ Ἀρσινοΐτου νομοῦ. ὁμολογεῖ Ἡρακλείδης ὁ καὶ Οὐαλέριος Ἡρακλείδου τοῦ Μάρωνος ἀναγραφόμενος ἐπʼ ἀμφόδου Ταμείων Πέρσης τῆς ἐπιγονῆς ὡς ἐτῶν τεσσεράκοντα ἕξ , οὐλὴ γόνατι ἀρισ τερῷ, Ταμύσθᾳ Διδύμου τοῦ καὶ Μυσθαρίωνος ὡς ἐτῶν τριάκον τα πέντε ἀσήμῳ μετὰ κυρίου τοῦ ἀνδρὸς Λυσιμάχου τοῦ Ἀπολλωνίου ὡς ἐτῶν τριάκοντα ὀκτώὀκτώι , οὐλὴ ποδὶ δεξιῷ, ἔχειν παρʼ αὐτῆς Ταμύσθας παραχρῆμα διὰ χειρὸς ἐξ οἴκου χρῆσιν ἔντοκον ἀργυρίου ἐπισήμου κεφαλαίου δραχμὰς διακοσίας δέκα δύο καὶ ἐπάναγκον τὸν ὁμολογοῦντα Ἡρακλείδην τὸν καὶ Οὐαλέριον ἀποδώσεινἀποδώσιν τῇ Ταμύσθᾳ τὰς τῆς χρήσεως ἀργυρίου κεφαλαίου δραχμὰς διακοσίας δέκα δύο καὶ τοὺς τόκους τῇ εἰκάδι τοῦ Τῦβι μηνὸς τοῦ ἐνεστῶτος ἑβδόμου ἔτους ἄνευ πάσης ὑπερθέσεως καὶ εὑρησιλογίαςεὑρησιλογείας, ἐὰν δὲ μὴ ἀπο δῷ καθʼ ἃ γέγραπταιγεγαπται, ἀποτεισάτωἀποτισάτω ὁ Ἡρακλείδης ὁ καὶ Οὐαλέριος τῇ Ταμύσθᾳ τὸ μὲν προκίμενον κεφάλαιον μεθʼ ἡμιολίας, τοὺς δὲ τόκους ἁπλοῦς, γεινομένης τῇ Ταμύσθᾳ τῆς πράξεως ἔκ τε τοῦ Ἡρακλείδου τοῦ καὶ Οὐαλερίου καὶ ἐκ τῶν ὑπαρχόντων αὐτῷ πάντων. Ἡρακλείδης ὁ καὶ Οὐαλέρις Ἡρακλείδου τοῦ Μάρωνος ἀπὸ Ταμείων ὁμολογῶ ἔ χειν παρὰ Ταμύσθας τῆς Διδύμου τοῦτο καὶ Μυσθαρίωνος διὰ χειρὸςχιρὸς ἐξ οἴκουὔκου χρῆσιν ἔντοκον ἀργυρίου κεφαλαίου δραχμὰς διακοσίας δέκα δύο , ἃς καὶ ἐπάναγκον ἀποδώσω τῇ εἰκάδι τοῦ ΤῦβιΤοῖβι μηνὸς τοῦ ἐνεστῶτος ἑβδόμου ἔτους ἀνυ περθέτως, γεινομένης αὐτῇ τῆς πράξεως ἔκ τʼ ἐμοῦ καὶ ἐκ τῶν ὑπαρχόν των μοι πάντων καθὼς πρόκειται. Ταμύσθα μετὰ κυρίου τοῦ ἀνδρὸς Λυσιμάχου γέγονε εἴς με ἡ ὁμολογία καθὼςκαθὸς πρόκιται.
sources.translation:
The 7th year of Imperator Caesar Trajans Hadrianus Augustus,
                      the month of August the 20th, at Tebtunis in the Polemon district of the Arsinoite nome.
                Herakleides also called Valerius son of Herakleides son of Maron, registered in the Treasuries
                      quarter, Persian of the epigone, about 46 years old, having a scar on the left knee,
                      acknowledges to Tamystha daughter of Didymos also called Mystharion, about 35 years
                      old, having no distinctive mark, with the assistance of her husband Lysimachos son of
                      Apollonios, about 38 years old, having a scar on the right foot, that he has received from
                      her, Tamystha, forthwith from hand to hand out of her house a loan of the capital sum of
                      212 drachmae of coined silver bearing interest;  and the acknowledging party Herakleides
                      also called Valerius is bound to repay to Tamystha the capital sum of the loan, say 212
                      dr., with the interest, on the 20th of the month of Tybi of the present 7th year without
                      any delay or subterfuge. If Herakleides also called Valerius fails to repay as is written,
                      he shall pay to Tamystha the aforesaid capital with a surplus of 50% and the simple
                      interest, Tamystha having the right of execution on Herakleides also called Valerius and
                      on all his property.  (Subscriptions of Herakleides-Valerius and of  Lysimachos on behalf
                      of his wife).
                      Translation: B.A. van Groningen, P.Fam. Tebt. 22

sources.description: Sale | Didyme sells 5.5 arourae for 2,000 dr.

sources.translation:
The 7th year of Imperator Caesar Trajanus Hadrianus Augustus,
                      Phamenoth 21st, at Tebtunis in the Polemon district of the Arsinoite nome. Didyme,
                      daughter of Lysimachos, son of Lysimachos, aged about 75 years, having a scar on the left
                      brow, with the assistance of Valerius, son of Herakleides, aged about 46 years, having a
                      scar on the right knee, acknowledges to Lysimachos, son of Didymos, son of Lysimachos,
                      aged about 68 years, having a scar on the right knee, that she has ceded to him, Lysimachos,
                      as well as to his descendants and their cessionaries, from the said day for ever, out of
                      the 10 1/2 1/16 arourae, with 1/4 aroura allotment to the village, total 10 1/2 1/4 1/16 arourae
                      (now) belonging to herself, Didyme, near the village of Ibion Eikosipentarouron in the
                      aforesaid district, and formerly to Herakleides, son of Maron, and assigned to her by a deed
                      of partition which she drew up with three partners in the 3rd year of Hadrian Caesar the
                      lord month of Pachon 28th through the registrar’s office here, the 5 1/2 1/64 arourae of
                      catoecic land, with 1/8 aroura allotment to the village, total 5 1/2 1/8 1/64 arourae situated
                      in the northern part of the whole, from west to east, and at the same time the entire proceeds
                      of them during the present 7th year and the fourth part of the watchtower there,
                      which belongs to the ceded arourae.  And from the ceded area, Lysimachos shall reserve for
                      the arourae in the south which remain Didyme’s property, a drying place in the south-west
                      of the tower, 4 square ammata in the superficies, for the - - -, and the crops which will be sown
                      on them. Neighbours of the ceded arourae are, as they have unanimously declared: in the
                      south, the arourae which remain Didyme’s property; in the north, the lot belonging to - - -
                      and Lysimachos and Arios, the three sons of Apollonios; in the west, a field belonging
                      to the state behind which there is a canal irrigating the ceded arourae; in the east, another
                      canal. - - - all the rights of irrigation Didyme is entitled to by virtue of the aforesaid deed
                      of partition. Valid shall also be the transactions registered in the list of katalochismoi of
                      the katoikoi.  The acknowledging party, Didyme, has received from Lysimachos forthwith
                      from hand to hand out of his house the whole sum of 2000 silver drachmae fixed for the
                      cession of the ceded 5 1/2 1/64 arourae with the allotment; and Didyme and her cessionaries
                      shall guarantee with full security for Lysimachos and his cessionaries the aforesaid ceded
                      property, which she transfers neither encumbered, nor liable to distraint, nor mortgaged,
                      and free of all public duties, artaba-taxes, naubia and any kind of payments due for the
                      past up to the former 6th year included, as well as from private obligations and every
                      claim whensoever. She shall neither accuse nor dispute nor prosecute with a view to the
                      aforesaid agreement, and if she prosecutes or accuses, the prosecution and accusation shall
                      not be valid;  and Didyme shall pay to Lysimachos, if she does not guarantee or transfer
                      forthwith, the cession sum which she received, with a surplus of 50%, twice the amount
                      of the damage and a penalty of 1000 silver drachmae, besides to the Treasury the same
                      sum and nothing less, apart from the fact that the agreement shall remain valid and unchanged
                      forever - - - the cession shall be valid.  Registered by Lourios also called Apollonios,
                      chief of the registrar’s office of Tebtunis and Kerkesouchon Oros, in the 7th year
                      of Hadrian Caesar the lord, 27th of Phamenoth.
              To Dionysios and Herakleides, ex-gymnasiarchs, keepers of the archives of enkteseis
                      in the Arsinoite nome, from Lysimachos, son of Didymos, son of Lysimachos, registered
                      in the Tharapeia quarter. As a supplement to the returns presented to your predecessors,
                      I herewith return the cateocic land which has today been transferred to me with your
                      permission by Didyme, daughter of Lysimachos, son of Lysimachos, registered in the
                      Treasuries quarter, who had delivered a return to your predecessors, viz. 5 1/2 1/64 arourae
                      and 1/8 aroura allotment, total area 5 1/2 1/8 1/64 arourae, reaching from west to east in the
                      northern part of 10 1/2 1/4 1/16 arourae near Ibion Eikosipentarouron, free from every debt
                      and mortgage, at the price of 2000 drachmae.  Therefore, I present this return. - - -
                      ex-gymnasiarch through Apollonios secretary. Entered in the register. 7th year of Hadrian
                      Caesar the lord, Phamenoth 27th.
                      Translation: B.A. van Groningen, P.Fam. Tebt. 23

Πτολεμαῖος Ἡρακλείδου Ὀσιραντινόειος ὁ κ αὶ Κλειτ ό ριος οἰκῶ ν οἰκο ἐν τῷ το βῆτα γράμματι πλινθείῳ . ὀμνύω τὸν προκείμενον ὅρκ ον γνωστεύειν τὸν προγεγραμμένον προγεγραμμένων παῖδα Ἡρακλείδην τὸν καὶ Οὐαλέριον ἡμερῶν ε ἴκοσι ε ἴκοσει γεννηθέντα τῷ το Λυσιμάχῳ Λυοιμάχῳ τῷ καὶ Διδύμῳ Ἀντινοεῖ ἐκ τῆς αὐτοῦ γυναι κ ὸς Νινναροῦτος Ὀρσενούφεως Ἀντι νοΐδος ἢ ἔνοχος εἴην τῷ ὅρκῳ.
sources.translation: [Translation from P. L. Bat. 6] To ---, nomarch of Antinoopolis, from Lysimachos also called Didymos son of Herakleides, of the Matidian tribe and Callitecnian deme, living in his own house in the alpha quarter, 7th block. Petronius Mamertinus, former prefect, made known to us the benefits granted to us by the deified Hadrianus, the founder of our city, by which he directed that the children of Antinoites who are returned by us, their parents, within thirty days of their birth should be maintained from the proceeds of funds granted by him for this purpose and from other revenues. I therefore return the son born to me, Herakleides also called Valerius, twenty days old, by my wife Ninnarous daughter of Orsenouphis, Antinoite, the fee for whose aparche I paid through the most excellent senate, furnishing three guarantors of the marriage and the parentage, Lysanias son of Didymos, of the Matidian tribe and Plotinian deme, Didymos son of Didymos, of the Matidian tribe and Callitecnian deme, and Ptolemaios son of Herakleides, of the Osirantinoan tribe and Clitorian deme. And I swear by the Fortune of Imperator Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antonius Augustus Pius and the most great god Osirantinous that the foregoing particulars are true, or may I be subject to the consequences of the oath. (Date and subscriptions of the father and the guarantors). [Bell].

sources.translation: Copy of a petition. To Valerius also known as Philantinoos and Philantinoos also known as Herodes, both sons of Philantinoos also known as Nilammon, and to Herakleia and Didyme and Philoserapis, the three [children] of Lysimachos also known as Didymos, citizens of Antinoopolis, from Pisais son of Diogas, from the village of Kerkesucha Orus of the Polemon division. I wish to lease from you the catoecic allotment belonging to you [...].

sources.translation:
Copy of the minutes of Apollonides, strategos of the Polemon district, 2nd year of Imperator Caesar Trajanus Hadrianus Augustus, 
                Pharmouthi 11th, in the court.  Dieras, son of Ischyrion, represented by Ammonios, his advocate, having said that he had lent to Isidora, daughter of Lysimachos, 
                a small sum of money, but had not recovered it, that the (therefore) proceeded to distraint and execution and the consequent legal measures and brought everything to an end; 
                that the matter was brought before Cascelius Geminus, judicial officer, because he recalcitrated; that he (Geminus), deciding about the matter, 
                also arrested her son, Kronion, in order that they should submit to the verdict; that they, (however), after the legal measures and the minutes which are against tehm, 
                once more gather the fruits of the estate, concerning which the legal measures have been fully taken; 
                Isidora having through her advocate Sueros that she will pay to those who give up (their claim) (?), if she meets with indulgence, 
                but that he (Dieras) has snatched away two more arourae which were pledged to another creditor, 
                that Dieras unduly asserts that he has taken the legal steps as well concerning these two arourae as concerning the others, 
                and now claims to recover the debt even without delay - - - make restitution of the arourae; and Isidora having asked for a time-limit. 
                 The strategos (said): let the minutes of Cascelius Geminus be read. When these had been read, dated in the 10th year of the deified Trajan, Mecheir 4th, 
                (he said): according to the minutes concerning the question and the fact that Kronion, son of the debtor, was arrested with her, 
                it seems reasonable to me that the whole debt will be paid back to Dieras tomorrow.  And if they do not pay back, 
                I am of opinion that the legal decisions must be valid and unshaken. And the debtors shall have no claim on the ground. 
                Dieras (said): if only they will not collect the fruits once more, after your decision! Kronion (said): we ran into expenses at our own costs. 
                The strategos (said): the village-scribe shall ascertain how large the expenses have been and he will take care that you get them back when he 
                (Dieras) gathers the summer-harvest. I have read.
                Translation: B.A. van Groningen, P.Fam. Tebt. 19

sources.greek: Λουκίωι Τρεβίωι Πρόκλω Λουκίωι Τρεβίωι Πρόκλω ι τῶι κρατίστωι ἐ π ιστρατήγωι παρὰ Φιλίππου καὶ Χαριτίου ἀμφοτέρ παρὰ Φιλίππου καὶ Χαριτίου ἀμφοτέρ ω ν Ἀφροδισίου τοῦ Φ ι λίππου κατοίκου τῶν ἐν τῷ Ἀρσινο τῶν ἐν τῷ Ἀρσινο ΐ τῃ ἀνδρῶν Ἑλλήνων Ϛυοε , τῆς δὲ Χαριτίου μετὰ κυρίου ἐμοῦ τοῦ Φιλίππου. ἡ ἀμφοτέρων μήτηρ Διδυμάριον ἐτελεύτησεν ἔτι ἀπὸ Φιλίππου. ἡ ἀμφοτέρων μήτηρ Διδυμάριον ἐτελεύτησεν ἔτι ἀπὸ ........ ἔτους θεοῦ Ἁδριανοῦ ἐπὶ κληρο θεοῦ Ἁδριανοῦ ἐπὶ κληρο νό μοις ἡμῖν ἡμεῖν τότε ἀφήλιξι, κατὰ δ ὲ τοὺς νόμους ἐλάχομεν τὰ ὑπάρχοντα καὶ οἰκ τὰ ὑπάρχοντα καὶ οἰκ ό πεδα καὶ δουλικὰ σώματα τέσσερα κ αὶ τ ὰ τούτων ἔγγο να καὶ ἀπόθετα να καὶ ἀπόθετα ἅπαντα . ὁ δὲ προγεγραμμένος ἡ μ ῶ ν πατὴρ Ἀφροδί σιο σιο ς πάντων ἀντεφαπτόμενος , ὕστερον, ἐπεὶ ἐπὶ σ υ νῆλθεν Σαρα πιάδι τῇ καὶ Ἀ θηναρίῳ, καὶ ὡς θηναρίῳ, καὶ ὡς ... κη α . ... π άντων ἐγένετο ἀλόγως ἀξιῶ ν ............. τῶν ἰδίων μηδὲν ἅ τῶν ἰδίων μηδὲν ἅ μα τε καταφρονῶν τῆς περὶ ἡμ ᾶς ἀβ οηθήτου ἀσθενείας . ἔκτοτε οὖν μέχρι το ἔκτοτε οὖν μέχρι το ῦ ν ῦν πάντων ἀντιλαμβανομένου κ αὶ ἀντιποιουμέ νου καὶ τῶν σωμάτων νου καὶ τῶν σωμάτων τ ὰς ἀποφορὰς ἐκλεγομένου ἐγλεγομένου , πάντ α καταγραφῆναι συνέταξεν εἰς τὸ τῆς γυναικὸς αὐτοῦ ὄν συνέταξεν εἰς τὸ τῆς γυναικὸς αὐτοῦ ὄν ο μα, καίτοι τοῦ πατρὸ ς ἡμῶν σιτοπαρα λήμπτου γενομένου καὶ εἰς ἄπορον λήμπτου γενομένου καὶ εἰς ἄπορον τραπ έ ντος τραπ ε ντων . αὐτοί τε οὖν ἡμεῖς ἄποροι γε νάμενοι διὰ τὸ καὶ ἐμὲ τὸν Φίλιππον ἐν δημοσίαις χρεία ι ς γεγον έναι καὶ ἀναγ καίως τὴν ἐπὶ σὲ τὸν εὐεργέτην καταφυγὴν ποιούμενο καίως τὴν ἐπὶ σὲ τὸν εὐεργέτην καταφυγὴν ποιούμενο ι ἀξιοῦμεν τὰ τὰ ἴ δια ἡμῖν ἡμεῖν ἀποκατασταθῆναι σὺν ταῖς τοῦ παντὸς χρ ήσεσιν, ἔτι δὲ καὶ τῶν σωμάτων καὶ τῶν σωμάτων τῇ ἐπιγονῇ, ἵνα καὶ ὦμεν ὑπὸ σ ο ῦ βεβοηθημένοι. διευτύχει . Φίλιππος καὶ Χαρίτιον ἀμφότεροι Ἀφροδεισίου ἐπιδεδώκαμεν ἐπιδεδόκαμεν . Φίλ ι ππος ἔγραψα καὶ ὑ πὲρ τῆς ἀδελφῆς μὴ εἰδυίης ἠδοίης γράμματα. ἔτους ιδ Αὐτοκράτορος Καίσαρος Τ ίτου Αἰλίου Ἁδριανοῦ Ἀντωνείνου Σεβαστ Ἀντωνείνου Σεβαστ ο ῦ Εὐσ εβοῦ ς Μεσορὴ κγ . ἔτους ιδ Μεσορὴ κγ . μετὰ κατασπορὰν ἔντυχέ μοι. μετὰ κατασπορὰν ἔντυχέ μοι.
sources.translation:
An Lucius Trebius Proculus, den vir egregius und Epistrategen,
                      von Philippos und Charition, beide Kinder des Aphrodisios, des Sohnes des
                      Philippos, des Katöken und eines der 6475 Faijum-Griechen, Charition zugleich
                      unter meiner, des Philippos, Assistenz als Geschlechtsvormund.  Unser beider
                      Mutter Didymarion ist schon seit dem .. Jahre des vergötterten Hadrian verstorben
                      und wir, die wir damals noch unmündig waren, wurden ihre Erben und
                      erhielten gemäß den Gesetzen ihren ländlichen Grundbesitz und die Hausplätze
                      und vier Sklavinnen mit ihrer Nachkommenschaft und ...... Verwahrgut.  Unser
                      vorgenannter Vater Aphrodisios aber erhob Einspruch (?) gegen alles und später,
                      nachdem er Sarapias, die auch Athenarion heißt, geheiratet hatte, wurde er
                      ...... von allem, indem er unberechtigterweise verlangte, nichts von unserem
                      Eigentum [solle uns zurückgegeben werden?], und zugleich unserer hülflosen
                      Schwachheit nicht achtete.  Und indem er nun seitdem bis jetzt alles festhält
                      und beansprucht und die Erträge aus den Dienstleistungen der Sklaven für sich
                      fordert, hat er (jetzt) das ganze Vermögen überschreiben lassen auf den Namen
                      seiner Frau, obwohl er, unser Vater liturgischer Beamter der Speicherverwaltung
                      gewesen und infolgedessen vermögenslos geworden war.  Da wir nun selbst bedürftig
                      geworden sind(?), weil auch ich Philippos mich in Staatsliturgien befunden
                      habe, nehmen wir notgedrungen zu Dir dem Wohltäter unsere Zuflucht und
                      beantragen, unser Eigen uns zurückzuerstatten mitsamt den vollen Nutzungen und
                      außerdem auch dem Zuwachs an Sklaven, damit uns durch Dich geholfen werde.
                      Lebewohl.  (2.Hd.) Philippos und Charition, beide Kinder des Aphrodisios, wir
                      haben die Eingabe eingereicht. Ich Philippos habe auch für meine schreibunkundige
                      Schwester geschrieben. Jahr 14 des Imperator Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus
                      Antoninus Augustus Pius am 23. Mesore.
                      (3.Hd.) Jahr 14 am 23. Mesore.
              (4.Hd.) Nach der Aussaat wende Dich an mich.
                      Translation: P.M. Meyer, P.Meyer 8

"Lysimachos son of Didymos son of Lysimachos"

bio: "Village moneylender active from at least 89 CE. Lent cash and seed-grain to farmers and 'Persians of the Epigone' from his house in Tebtunis. Used his own private measuring standard for grain loans."
disambiguation: "Generation 1 patriarch. Not to be confused with Lysimachos also called Didymos (generation 3), his great-grandson."

bio: "Borrowed 36 silver drachmas from Lysimachos son of Didymos in 92 CE, providing mutual surety with his wife Tepheros and son Orsenouphis. Described as 'Persian of the Epigone,' age 47, with a scar on the left wrist."
disambiguation: "Not the Chairemon son of Akousilaos who borrowed wheat in 94 CE."
bio: "Borrowed 112 drachmas from Lysimachos son of Didymos in 98-99 CE. Later defaulted and was pursued in court by creditor Dieras son of Ischyrion in 118 CE. Her son Kronion was arrested."
disambiguation: "Possibly related to the Lysimachos family but the patronymic was common."

bio: "Father of Herakleides/Valerius, Lysimachos, and Didymarion. Married Didyme daughter of Lysimachos. Served as keeper of public archives, later fined 282 dr. for irregularities. Settled inheritance claims spanning decades."
disambiguation: "Generation 1. Father of the Antinoite citizen Herakleides/Valerius."
bio: "Received 401 drachmas 2 obols from Herakleides son of Maron as his one-third share of a testamentary obligation dating to 86-87 CE. Son of Apollonarion daughter of Lysimachos."
bio: "Mother of Herakleides son of Maron. Created a testamentary agreement granting 2,000 drachmas to Apollonarion daughter of Lysimachos in 86-87 CE."
bio: "Mother of Dioskoros/Kastor. Received a 2,000 drachma testamentary grant from Thaubarion daughter of Mysthos. Half-sister of Herakleides son of Maron."

bio: "Wife of Herakleides son of Maron. Owned land at Theogonis. Mother of Herakleides/Valerius, Lysimachos, and Didymarion. Gave daughter golden earrings with pearls and a purple garment in 122 CE. Sold land at age 75 in 123 CE."
disambiguation: "Generation 1. Not to be confused with Didymarion (her daughter)."

bio: "Married Lysimachos son of Herakleides through the grapheion of Tebtunis. Divorced in 113-114 CE at about age 20, with her father Kastor as guardian. Dowry returned in full. Left behind a son, Herakleides also called Valerius."
bio: "Father of Kronous/Philoumene. Served as her guardian during divorce proceedings in 113-114 CE. Age 44 at the time. Acknowledged return of dowry."
bio: "Married Kronous/Philoumene, daughter of Kastor. Divorced in 113-114 CE at age 30. Father of Herakleides also called Valerius."
disambiguation: "Generation 2. Son of Herakleides son of Maron. Not to be confused with the moneylender Lysimachos son of Didymos."
canonical_name: "Herakleides also called Valerius son of Herakleides"
"Herakleides son of Sarapion son of Herakleides"

bio: "Central figure of generation 2. First married Apia/Herakleia (who died), then Herakleia daughter of Hermos. Became Antinoite citizen c. 130 CE. Registered sons in 133 CE at age 57. Managed land and property across Tebtunis and Antinoopolis."
disambiguation: "The father. Not to be confused with his grandson Herakleides also called Valerius son of Lysimachos/Didymos (b. 151 CE)."

bio: "Daughter of Kronion (brother of Lysimachos son of Didymos). Lived in unwritten marriage with Herakleides son of Sarapion. Became pregnant, gave birth, and died. Her death triggered a dispute over the child's identity."
canonical_name: "Herakleia also called Isidora daughter of Mysthos"
bio: "Mother of Apia/Herakleia. Petitioned the archidikastes alleging that Herakleides had substituted her dead grandchild with an impostor. The dispute was settled after the infant died."
disambiguation: "Not Isidora daughter of Lysimachos (the debtor)."
patronymic: "Hermos (also called Hermous son of Didymos)"

bio: "Wife of Herakleides also called Valerius. Owned slave Athenarion and authorized her sale in 132 CE. Pursued debt enforcement through the archidikastes in 133 CE. Mother of Lysimachos/Didymos, Philosarapis, and Philantinoos."
canonical_name: "Lysimachos also called Didymos son of Herakleides"

bio: "Registered as Antinoite citizen at age 7 in 133 CE. Registered his own son's birth in 151 CE, invoking Hadrian's child welfare fund. Petitioned with brother Philosarapis against brother Philantinoos over pledged slave Martilla in 168 CE."
disambiguation: "Generation 3. Not to be confused with the moneylender Lysimachos son of Didymos (generation 1)."
bio: "Registered as Antinoite citizen at age 1 in 133 CE. Underwent epikrisis as ephebe at age 14 (c. 146-161 CE). Co-petitioned with brother against Philantinoos over slave Martilla in 168 CE."
disambiguation: "Generation 3 son. Not to be confused with Philosarapis son of Lysimachos (generation 4)."
bio: "Brother of Lysimachos/Didymos and Philosarapis. Recklessly pledged the family's jointly owned maternal slave Martilla to a creditor in the Arsinoite, pocketing the loan money."
bio: "Jointly owned maternal slave woman inherited by three brothers. Pledged unilaterally by Philantinoos to a creditor in the Arsinoite without his brothers' knowledge."
bio: "Wife of Lysimachos also called Didymos. Mother of Herakleides also called Valerius (b. January 151 CE). Identified as an Antinoite, indicating the family married within the citizen class."
canonical_name: "Herakleides also called Valerius son of Lysimachos/Didymos"
bio: "Born January 151 CE, registered 20 days later. Enrolled in Hadrian's child welfare program. His birth registration includes the oath by Osirantinoos."
disambiguation: "Generation 4 infant. Not the older Herakleides also called Valerius (b. c. 76 CE)."

bio: "Received child support payments (tropheia) from Philosarapis son of Lysimachos for the maintenance of minor Ioulios/Herodes. Her father was Herakleides also called Didymos, a Greek from the Arsinoite."

bio: "Guardian of the minor Ioulios/Herodes, paying child support to Herakleia/Arsinoe. Hired musicians for a 6-day Tebtunis festival in 223 CE. Managed grain deliveries in 224 CE. The last documented member of the family."
disambiguation: "Generation 4. Not to be confused with Philosarapis son of Herakleides (generation 3 ephebe)."
bio: "Minor child whose maintenance was managed by guardian Philosarapis son of Lysimachos and received by Herakleia also called Arsinoe."
canonical_name: "Marcus Aurelius Didymos also called Alexandros"
bio: "Brother of Aurelia Herakleia also called Arsinoe. Acted as her representative in receiving child support payments in 218 CE."
bio: "Aulos-player hired by Philosarapis son of Lysimachos with three other performers for a 6-day festival at Tebtunis. Wage: 18 drachmas per day plus allowances."
bio: "Granary official at Tebtunis who certified delivery of 6 artabae of wheat through Philosarapis in 224 CE. The last document in the archive."
bio: "Had grain delivered through Philosarapis in 224 CE. Identified by his mother Artemidora rather than his father, an unusual formula."
bio: "Daughter of Herakleides son of Maron and Didyme. Received 600 drachmas above her dowry and golden earrings with pearls from her mother in 122 CE. Illiterate."

description: "Village in the southern Fayum, site of the temple of Soknebtunis (crocodile god). One of the best-documented villages in Greco-Roman Egypt, with an estimated 100,000 papyri discovered. Home to a grapheion (notary office), bathhouses, and a significant literary tradition in Greek and Demotic."

description: "City founded by Hadrian in 130 CE in honor of his drowned companion Antinous. Offered privileged citizenship to Greek residents of the Arsinoite nome. Features included Greek-style tribal and deme organizations, an alimentary fund for children, and the cult of Osirantinoos (Osiris-Antinous)."
description: "Capital of the Arsinoite nome. Seat of the metropolitan grapheion and administrative center."
description: "Village near Tebtunis where the family owned kleros-land (allotment land). Mentioned in the earliest and latest documents of the archive."
description: "Village in the Polemon district near which vineyard land was sold in 92 CE."
description: "Village near which agricultural land was leased in 98 CE."
description: "Village where an agricultural lease was executed in 190 CE, with rent split between Varelios and Herakleia."
description: "Village where Didyme daughter of Lysimachos owned land, with taxes paid on her behalf by Herakleides son of Maron."

description: "Military governor of a nome division. In the Fayum, the strategos of the Polemon division handled disputes involving Tebtunis residents. Eudemos served as strategos during the Apia case (120-121 CE)."

description: "Chief judicial officer of Egypt. A priest who also served as the highest judge in civil cases. Received petitions and directed lower officials to act. Involved in the Apia dispute (P.Fam.Tebt. 20) and Herakleia's foreclosure proceedings (P.Fam.Tebt. 29)."
description: "Governor of a nome or city. At Antinoopolis, the nomarch received birth registrations (P.Fam.Tebt. 33) and petitions about property disputes (P.Fam.Tebt. 38)."
description: "Chairman of the city senate at Antinoopolis. Authorized citizenship registrations and aparche payments. Kastor son of Ammonios served as prytanis in 133 CE; Messius Junianus in 151 CE."
description: "Regional governor overseeing multiple nomes. Minicius Corellianus approved Philosarapis's ephebe registration (P.Fam.Tebt. 32)."

description: "Financial judges who adjudicated debt and property cases. Panels of three judges, originally itinerant. Titus Flavius Apollodoros and Poplienus adjudicated Herakleia's foreclosure case (P.Fam.Tebt. 29)."
description: "Granary official responsible for certifying grain deliveries and managing the village grain supply. Aurelius Pollion served as sitologos of Tebtunis in 224 CE (P.Fam.Tebt. 55)."

description: "Official responsible for maintaining public records. Herakleides son of Maron held this position and was later fined 282 drachmas for irregularities during his administration (P.Fam.Tebt. 14)."

description: "Male guardian required by law for women's legal transactions. Could be a father, husband, brother, or kinsman. Kastor son of Pyrion served as kyrios for his daughter's divorce (P.Fam.Tebt. 13); Lysimachos son of Lysaneios served for Herakleia's slave sale (P.Fam.Tebt. 27)."
description: "Guardian appointed for minor children. Philosarapis son of Lysimachos served as epitropos of the minor Ioulios/Herodes, managing child support payments (P.Fam.Tebt. 53a-c)."
